meta_title: For Founders: idea validation to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Idea Validation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop guessing. Learn to validate AI-powered SaaS ideas quickly. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch profitable products.
content.answer_block: Idea validation isn't about intuition; it's about friction mapping. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea and start building small, targeted solutions for niche problems. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can test market appetite in hours, not months, ensuring you build what people pay for.
content.intro:
The blank screen of an IDE is the graveyard of a thousand startups. You have the technical capability to build anything, but you’re paralyzed by the fear that what you build won't matter. It’s the classic trap: you’re searching for a 'billion-dollar' spark instead of hunting for real-world pain. The result is months of wasted code on a product that solves a problem nobody actually has.

But here is the secret: profitable SaaS products aren't 'found'—they are engineered through systematic validation. By shifting your focus from 'what should I build' to 'where is the friction,' you transform yourself from a coder into a founder. We are going to use the raw speed of Antigravity and the intelligence of Claude Code to strip away the guesswork and ship a validated MVP before the weekend is over.

steps.title: Uncover Niche Friction Points
steps.description: Don't brainstorm; observe. Analyze subreddits or industry forums for recurring 'How do I...' or 'I hate it when...' questions. Focus on manual tasks requiring Excel or repetitive data entry.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this CSV of forum discussions and identify the top 3 recurring manual tasks mentioned. Present them as potential SaaS opportunities ranked by pain intensity and technical feasibility.

steps.title: Rapid MVP Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate for a micro-SaaS. Keep it to a single landing page and one core utility function. Speed is your only metric here.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Next.js boilerplate with Supabase auth and Stripe integration. Include a simple form to collect user email and a 'Waitlist' status.

steps.title: Deploy the Smoke Test
steps.description: Deploy your landing page to Vercel immediately. The goal isn't a feature-complete app; it's a 'smoke test' to see if anyone is willing to enter their email.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that links this repository to Vercel and sets up environment variables for production readiness.

steps.title: Iterative Feedback Loops
steps.description: Once live, use Claude Code to analyze traffic logs and user behavior. If no one signs up, pivot the messaging or the core value prop immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the last 24 hours of landing page analytics. Suggest 3 A/B test variations for the hero headline to increase conversion.

steps.title: Monetization Validation
steps.description: Add a 'Pre-order' button. If a user clicks, redirect them to a Stripe Checkout link. Actual credit card entry is the only true validation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a Stripe checkout integration that triggers on the 'Join Waitlist' button and logs intent to a database.

pro_tips: Never build an auth system from scratch; use Clerk or Supabase to reclaim 20 hours of dev time.
The 'Pre-sell' is the best validation; if no one pays to reserve a spot, the problem isn't painful enough.
Avoid feature bloat by strictly adhering to a 'one-page, one-action' policy for the first 30 days.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests before the code; it keeps you honest about your MVP scope.
Target 'B2B' over 'B2C'—businesses pay for software that saves them money or time without needing emotional convincing.
content.next_steps: Once you have your first 10 signups, stop building and start interviewing. Call every single person who signed up and ask them why they did it. Their answers will dictate your product roadmap better than any feature list you could imagine.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping products that solve real problems. You have the tools, the process, and the expertise. Now you just need to execute. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
